message:
feat(cli): script runner, output modes, SQL highlighting, and dot-command surface

Turns the fsqlite-cli shell into something closer to a usable
sqlite3-style front end. Everything lands in main.rs — no new modules —
but the shell is no longer a thin prompt loop.

Command-line flags
------------------
* New `--init <path>` option that runs a script file through the shell
  before entering the interactive loop, mirroring `sqlite3 -init`.
* New `--batch` flag that forces `ShellOptions::forced_batch`, i.e. no
  prompts, no colorization, and `fail_on_error = true`, so that the CLI
  behaves predictably under pipe/redirect.
* `CliOptions` grows `init_path` and `force_batch` to carry the new
  flags through argument parsing.

Script execution
----------------
* New `execute_script_file` handles both `--init` and `.read` style
  nested invocations. Nested scripts inherit colorization and
  fail-on-error from the parent via `ShellOptions::nested_script`, so a
  nested `.read` does not silently flip interactive mode back on.
* `StatementScanState` + `last_sql_statement` + `sql_segment_has_tokens`
  track unterminated statements across script lines so the runner can
  accumulate multi-line SQL and only dispatch when a full statement is
  available.

Output rendering
----------------
* Introduces `OutputMode` { List | Column | Csv | Tabs | Line } and
  `OutputOptions { mode, headers }`, so results can be rendered in the
  standard sqlite3 output modes.
* `write_rows` dispatches to `write_delimited_rows`, `write_column_rows`,
  and `write_line_rows`. Column mode computes per-column widths and
  `format_column_line` pads values accordingly.
* Value/header rendering is split into `render_output_header`,
  `render_output_value`, `render_display_value`, `render_raw_value`,
  and `render_csv_field` so quoting and NULL/BLOB handling stay
  consistent across modes.
* `resolved_column_names` + `infer_result_column_names` let header rows
  populate from the prepared-statement metadata without re-parsing the
  SQL.

Prompt and interactive UX
-------------------------
* `render_prompt` now shows the active DB path and a preview of any
  pending (unterminated) statement via `render_pending_sql_preview` /
  `truncate_preview` so users can see what the shell thinks they are
  about to execute.
* `highlight_sql` + `push_colored_segment` + `is_sql_keyword` +
  `write_sql_statement` deliver light-weight ANSI SQL highlighting for
  interactive sessions. The new `ANSI_BOLD_BLUE`, `ANSI_GREEN`, and
  `ANSI_MAGENTA` constants feed this.

Dot commands
------------
* `write_tables`, `write_schema`, and `write_dump` implement `.tables`,
  `.schema`, and `.dump` directly against the connection (no detour
  through sqlite_master string parsing), with optional name filters.
* `parse_on_off` unifies `.headers on/off`, `.echo on/off`, etc.
* Identifier helpers (`is_identifier_start`, `is_identifier_continue`,
  `is_line_comment_start`, `format_row`) give the highlighter and
  statement scanner a shared notion of what counts as a token boundary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
